steps.title: Signal a replacement
steps.content: The first sentence says aircraft can no longer stay aloft by lift past the Kármán line. The next sentence explains the replacement mechanism: velocity keeps the aircraft in orbit even though the air is too thin for lift.

steps.title: Reject mismatched transitions
steps.content: Choice A suggests something remains true despite the prior idea, but the sentence is not a despite relationship. Choice B would introduce an example, and Choice C would introduce a concession; neither fits a replacement of lift by velocity. The needed transition signals substitution. The answer is D.
